These fees are charged each semester (Context #1). Therefore, the total amount due for the first year will include the tuition fee plus the semester‑based additional charges.

user_input: What is the deadline for session reporting at JKUAT?
retrieved_contexts:
Context #1:
2. Session Reporting Guidelines
Session reporting is the formal process by which a student confirms their continued enrolment in a given
semester. It is mandatory for all registered JKUAT students, including those on attachment or special
programmes.
n DEADLINE: Session reporting must be completed before the end of the FIRST WEEK of
every semester

Context #2:
. Failure to session-report by this deadline will result in the student being marked
absent for that semester and may lead to academic suspension.
2.1 How to Session Report
Step 1
Log in to the JKUAT Student Portal at portal.jkuat.ac.ke using your registration number
and password.
Step 2
Navigate to Academics → Session Reporting from the main menu.
Step 3
Select the current academic year and semester.
Step 4
Verify that your personal details and programme information are correct. Report any
discrepancies to the Registrar's Office immediately.
Step 5
Click 'Confirm Session Report'

Context #3:
will be sent by 15 June 2026.
• Full paper submission deadline: 25 June 2026 (for inclusion in the conference proceedings).
• Registration fee: KES 5,000 for students; KES 12,000 for faculty; USD 100 for international
participants.
• Virtual attendance option available via the JKUAT e-Conference platform for registered participants
unable to attend physically.
Note: For more details, visit the conference website or contact the Department of Mathematics, College of Pure and
Applied Sciences, JKUAT Main Campus, P.O
response: The deadlinefor session reporting is **before the end of the first week of each semester** (as stipulated in the Session Reporting Guidelines).
reference: By the end of the first week of every semester.

user_input: What happens if a student does not session report by the deadline?
retrieved_contexts:
Context #1:
. Failure to session-report by this deadline will result in the student being marked
absent for that semester and may lead to academic suspension.
2.1 How to Session Report
Step 1
Log in to the JKUAT Student Portal at portal.jkuat.ac.ke using your registration number
and password.
Step 2
Navigate to Academics → Session Reporting from the main menu.
Step 3
Select the current academic year and semester.
Step 4
Verify that your personal details and programme information are correct. Report any
discrepancies to the Registrar's Office immediately.
Step 5
Click 'Confirm Session Report'

Context #2:
2. Session Reporting Guidelines
Session reporting is the formal process by which a student confirms their continued enrolment in a given
semester. It is mandatory for all registered JKUAT students, including those on attachment or special
programmes.
n DEADLINE: Session reporting must be completed before the end of the FIRST WEEK of
every semester

Context #3:
. A confirmation message and reference number will be
displayed and emailed to your university email address.
Step 6
Print or save a copy of the session report confirmation for your records.
2.2 Important Reminders
• Session reporting is available online from the first day of each semester.
• Students on academic leave or deferred study must also session-report to confirm return.
• Manual session reporting at the Registrar's Office is available for students with portal access issues.
• Session reporting does not replace unit registration; both must be completed independently.
response: If a studentfails to submit the session report by the deadline, the student will be marked absent for that semester, and the university may place the student on academic suspension.
reference: The student is marked absent for that semester and may face academic suspension. 3

. The system will automatically block unit registration for any student whose fee payment is

3.2 Step-by-Step Unit Registration
Step 1
Ensure your semester fees are at least 75% paid. Check your fee balance on the Student

Step 3

Step 4

3. Unit Registration Procedure
Unit registration is the process of enrolling in specific course units for a given semester. Only registered
units will appear in your official academic transcript and examination schedule.
n DEADLINE: All unit registrations must be completed before the end of the FOURTH (4th)
WEEK of the semester. Late registration will not be permitted after this date without special
approval from the Dean of the relevant Faculty.
3.1 Fee Payment Requirement Before Unit Registration
n MANDATORY FEE PAYMENT THRESHOLD:
A student must have paid a minimum of 75% of the total semester fees before they can register
units

user_input: Will a student who has not cleared household fees be allowed to sit exams?
retrieved_contexts:
Context #1:
5. Examination Regulations & Fee Clearance
JKUAT maintains strict regulations governing student eligibility to sit for end-of-semester examinations.
Students must satisfy all the conditions below before being permitted into the examination room.
5.1 Household Fee Clearance
n CRITICAL REGULATION — HOUSEHOLD FEE:
A student who has not cleared their Household Fee (hostel / accommodation fee) in full will
NOT be permitted to sit for any University examinations for that semester.
This regulation applies to all students residing in JKUAT-managed accommodation

Context #2:
10

Registrar's Office with your student ID and fee receipt for manual assistance.

Context #3:
. Clearance must
be obtained from the Hostels & Accommodation Office and a clearance slip must be presented at
the examination venue together with the student identity card and unit registration card.
5.2 General Examination Eligibility Requirements
• Completed session reporting for the current semester.
• Registered all units for which examinations are to be sat.
• Attained a minimum class attendance of 75% for each registered unit.
• Cleared all outstanding household / hostel fees (for on-campus students).
• Submitted all continuous assessment tests (CATs) and assignments as required by the relevant
Department.
• Presented a valid Student Identity Card at the examination hall.
response: No. According to the Examination Regulations (Context #1) and the clearance requirement (Context #3), a student who has not cleared the household (hostel) fee is **not permitted** to sit for any university examinations for that semester. They must obtain a clearance slip from the Hostels & Accommodation Office before they can register for exams.
reference: No. Students who have not cleared household/hostel fees in full will not be permitted to sit any University examinations for that semester.
